sn.s: Bim Znacht fragt mich e Fründin über mich us.
sn.e: Over dinner a friend asks me about myself.
"Tell me, where exactly are you from?" she asks.
"I grew up in America," I say.

sn.s: "Han e Brüeder und e Schwöschter dehei."
sn.e: "I have a brother and a sister back home."

sn.s: "Und was machsch für e Arbet?" fragt sie.
sn.e: "And what work do you do?" she asks.
"I work as an engineer," I answer.

sn.s: "Spannend! Und was machsch i de Freiziit?"
sn.e: "Interesting! And what do you do in your free time?"
"I like hiking and read a lot."

sn.s: "Denn passt Luzärn perfekt zu dir!" lacht sie.
sn.e: "Then Lucerne suits you perfectly!" she laughs.

sn.s: Über mich z'rede uf Dütsch wird liechter.
sn.e: Talking about myself in German is getting easier.

sn.s: Ich cha mis Läbe erzähle, mini Gschicht.
sn.e: I can tell my life, my story.

sn.s: Es Gschpröch über sich säuber verbindet.
sn.e: A conversation about oneself connects people.

sn.s: So lärned d'Lüt, wär ich würkli bi.
sn.e: This way people learn who I really am.
